description: Spider风格通用NL2SQL测试集 - 5个Schema共100条

test_cases.question: How many employees are there in total?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM employees

test_cases.question: List all department names
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT dept_name FROM departments

test_cases.question: Find all female employees
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT first_name, last_name FROM employees WHERE gender = 'F'

test_cases.question: Show employees with salary greater than 10000
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, salary FROM employees WHERE salary > 10000

test_cases.question: What is the average salary of all employees?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT AVG(salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees

test_cases.question: Show employees ordered by hire date from earliest to latest
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, hire_date FROM employees ORDER BY hire_date ASC

test_cases.question: How many employees are in each department?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT d.dept_name, COUNT(*) AS emp_count FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.dept_id GROUP BY d.dept_name

test_cases.question: What is the average salary per department?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT d.dept_name, AVG(e.salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.dept_id GROUP BY d.dept_name

test_cases.question: Find the department with the highest total salary
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT d.dept_name, SUM(e.salary) AS total_salary FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.dept_id GROUP BY d.dept_name ORDER BY total_salary DESC LIMIT 1

test_cases.question: Which departments have more than 3 employees?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT d.dept_name, COUNT(*) AS emp_count FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.dept_id GROUP BY d.dept_name HAVING emp_count > 3

test_cases.question: Find employees who received a bonus in January 2024
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, sp.bonus FROM employees e JOIN salary_payments sp ON e.emp_id = sp.emp_id WHERE sp.pay_date BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2024-01-31' AND sp.bonus > 0

test_cases.question: Find the employee with the highest total compensation including bonuses
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(sp.amount + sp.bonus) AS total_comp FROM employees e JOIN salary_payments sp ON e.emp_id = sp.emp_id GROUP BY e.emp_id, e.first_name, e.last_name ORDER BY total_comp DESC LIMIT 1

test_cases.question: Find departments where average salary exceeds the company average
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT d.dept_name, AVG(e.salary) AS dept_avg FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.dept_id GROUP BY d.dept_name HAVING dept_avg > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees)

test_cases.question: Show the youngest and oldest employee in each department
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT d.dept_name, MIN(e.age) AS youngest, MAX(e.age) AS oldest FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.dept_id GROUP BY d.dept_name

test_cases.question: Count male and female employees
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT gender, COUNT(*) AS count FROM employees GROUP BY gender

test_cases.question: Find employees who earn more than their department average
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary, d.dept_name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.dept_id WHERE e.salary > (SELECT AVG(e2.salary) FROM employees e2 WHERE e2.dept_id = e.dept_id)

test_cases.question: What is the maximum salary?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT MAX(salary) AS max_salary FROM employees

test_cases.question: List the top 5 highest paid employees
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, salary FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 5

test_cases.question: For each department show the salary range and the number of employees earning above the department median
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT d.dept_name, MIN(e.salary) AS min_sal, MAX(e.salary) AS max_sal, MAX(e.salary) - MIN(e.salary) AS salary_range, COUNT(*) AS total_emp FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.dept_id GROUP BY d.dept_name ORDER BY salary_range DESC

test_cases.question: Find all employees hired in 2021
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, hire_date FROM employees WHERE YEAR(hire_date) = 2021

test_cases.question: How many students are there?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM students

test_cases.question: List all course names and their credits
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT course_name, credits FROM courses

test_cases.question: Show students with GPA above 3.5
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT name, gpa FROM students WHERE gpa > 3.5

test_cases.question: What is the average GPA of all students?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT AVG(gpa) AS avg_gpa FROM students

test_cases.question: How many students are in each major?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT major, COUNT(*) AS student_count FROM students GROUP BY major

test_cases.question: What is the average grade for each course?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT c.course_name, AVG(e.grade) AS avg_grade FROM enrollments e JOIN courses c ON e.course_id = c.course_id GROUP BY c.course_name

test_cases.question: Which student has the highest GPA?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT name, gpa FROM students ORDER BY gpa DESC LIMIT 1

test_cases.question: List all courses taught by Prof. Wang
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT c.course_name FROM courses c JOIN instructors i ON c.instructor_id = i.instructor_id WHERE i.name = 'Prof. Wang'

test_cases.question: Find students who are enrolled in more than 2 courses in 2024-Spring
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT s.name, COUNT(*) AS course_count FROM students s JOIN enrollments e ON s.student_id = e.student_id WHERE e.semester = '2024-Spring' GROUP BY s.student_id, s.name HAVING course_count > 2

test_cases.question: Find the course with the highest average grade
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT c.course_name, AVG(e.grade) AS avg_grade FROM enrollments e JOIN courses c ON e.course_id = c.course_id GROUP BY c.course_name ORDER BY avg_grade DESC LIMIT 1

test_cases.question: Find students whose grade is below the average grade of their course
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT s.name, c.course_name, e.grade FROM enrollments e JOIN students s ON e.student_id = s.student_id JOIN courses c ON e.course_id = c.course_id WHERE e.grade < (SELECT AVG(e2.grade) FROM enrollments e2 WHERE e2.course_id = e.course_id)

test_cases.question: How many courses does each instructor teach?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT i.name, COUNT(*) AS course_count FROM instructors i JOIN courses c ON i.instructor_id = c.instructor_id GROUP BY i.instructor_id, i.name

test_cases.question: List all instructors and their departments
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT name, department FROM instructors

test_cases.question: For each major show the average GPA and the number of students
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT major, COUNT(*) AS student_count, AVG(gpa) AS avg_gpa FROM students GROUP BY major ORDER BY avg_gpa DESC

test_cases.question: Find the top 3 students by total credits earned with a passing grade above 60
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT s.name, SUM(c.credits) AS total_credits FROM students s JOIN enrollments e ON s.student_id = e.student_id JOIN courses c ON e.course_id = c.course_id WHERE e.grade >= 60 GROUP BY s.student_id, s.name ORDER BY total_credits DESC LIMIT 3

test_cases.question: Show all Computer Science courses with their instructor names
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT c.course_name, i.name AS instructor_name FROM courses c JOIN instructors i ON c.instructor_id = i.instructor_id WHERE c.department = 'Computer Science'

test_cases.question: List students ordered by GPA descending
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT name, gpa FROM students ORDER BY gpa DESC

test_cases.question: Count enrollments per semester
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT semester, COUNT(*) AS enrollment_count FROM enrollments GROUP BY semester

test_cases.question: Find students who took courses in both Computer Science and Mathematics departments
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT s.name FROM students s JOIN enrollments e ON s.student_id = e.student_id JOIN courses c ON e.course_id = c.course_id WHERE c.department = 'Computer Science' AND s.student_id IN (SELECT e2.student_id FROM enrollments e2 JOIN courses c2 ON e2.course_id = c2.course_id WHERE c2.department = 'Mathematics')

test_cases.question: For each department show the number of courses and average enrollment count per course
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT c.department, COUNT(DISTINCT c.course_id) AS course_count, COUNT(e.enrollment_id) / COUNT(DISTINCT c.course_id) AS avg_enrollment FROM courses c LEFT JOIN enrollments e ON c.course_id = e.course_id GROUP BY c.department

test_cases.question: How many products are there?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM products

test_cases.question: List all category names
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT category_name FROM categories

test_cases.question: Show the most expensive product
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT product_name, price FROM products ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 1

test_cases.question: List all customers from Beijing
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT name, email FROM customers WHERE city = 'Beijing'

test_cases.question: How many products are in each category?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT c.category_name, COUNT(*) AS product_count FROM products p JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id GROUP BY c.category_name

test_cases.question: What is the total revenue from completed orders?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT SUM(total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed'

test_cases.question: Which customer has the most orders?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT c.name, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name ORDER BY order_count DESC LIMIT 1

test_cases.question: What is the average order amount per customer?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT c.name, AVG(o.total_amount) AS avg_order FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name

test_cases.question: Find the top 3 best selling products by quantity
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_sold FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name ORDER BY total_sold DESC LIMIT 3

test_cases.question: Show revenue by category from completed orders
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT cat.category_name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories cat ON p.category_id = cat.category_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY cat.category_name ORDER BY revenue DESC

test_cases.question: Find customers who have never placed an order
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT c.name FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.order_id IS NULL

test_cases.question: List all Nike products with their prices
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT product_name, price FROM products WHERE brand = 'Nike'

test_cases.question: Show monthly order count and total revenue for 2024
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT MONTH(order_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS order_count, SUM(total_amount) AS monthly_revenue FROM orders WHERE YEAR(order_date) = 2024 GROUP BY MONTH(order_date) ORDER BY month

test_cases.question: How many orders have pending status?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS pending_count FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending'

test_cases.question: Show all VIP customers with level 2 or above
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT name, city, vip_level FROM customers WHERE vip_level >= 2

test_cases.question: Find the customer who spent the most money across all their orders
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT c.name, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_spent FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 1

test_cases.question: Count orders by status
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS count FROM orders GROUP BY status

test_cases.question: Find products that have never been ordered
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT p.product_name FROM products p LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id WHERE oi.item_id IS NULL

test_cases.question: Show all orders placed in March 2024
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT o.order_id, c.name, o.order_date, o.total_amount, o.status FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE o.order_date BETWEEN '2024-03-01' AND '2024-03-31'

test_cases.question: For each city show the number of customers, total orders, and total revenue
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT c.city, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS customer_count, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count, SUM(o.total_amount) AS total_revenue FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.city ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

test_cases.question: How many artists are there?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM artists

test_cases.question: List all genres
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT genre_name FROM genres

test_cases.question: Show all artists from the UK
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT name FROM artists WHERE country = 'UK'

test_cases.question: How many albums does each artist have?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT a.name, COUNT(*) AS album_count FROM artists a JOIN albums al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name

test_cases.question: What is the total duration of each album in minutes?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT al.title, SUM(t.duration_seconds) / 60 AS total_minutes FROM albums al JOIN tracks t ON al.album_id = t.album_id GROUP BY al.album_id, al.title

test_cases.question: Show all Pop albums
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT al.title, a.name AS artist FROM albums al JOIN artists a ON al.artist_id = a.artist_id JOIN genres g ON al.genre_id = g.genre_id WHERE g.genre_name = 'Pop'

test_cases.question: Find the longest track across all albums
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT t.title, al.title AS album, t.duration_seconds FROM tracks t JOIN albums al ON t.album_id = al.album_id ORDER BY t.duration_seconds DESC LIMIT 1

test_cases.question: How many tracks are there per genre?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT g.genre_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count FROM genres g JOIN albums al ON g.genre_id = al.genre_id JOIN tracks t ON al.album_id = t.album_id GROUP BY g.genre_name ORDER BY track_count DESC

test_cases.question: List all tracks from the album Abbey Road
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT t.title, t.track_number, t.duration_seconds FROM tracks t JOIN albums al ON t.album_id = al.album_id WHERE al.title = 'Abbey Road' ORDER BY t.track_number

test_cases.question: Find artists who have released albums in more than one genre
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT a.name, COUNT(DISTINCT al.genre_id) AS genre_count FROM artists a JOIN albums al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name HAVING genre_count > 1

test_cases.question: Show all albums ordered by release year
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT title, release_year FROM albums ORDER BY release_year

test_cases.question: Count albums by genre
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT g.genre_name, COUNT(*) AS album_count FROM albums al JOIN genres g ON al.genre_id = g.genre_id GROUP BY g.genre_name

test_cases.question: Show the average track duration per artist
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT a.name, AVG(t.duration_seconds) AS avg_duration FROM artists a JOIN albums al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id JOIN tracks t ON al.album_id = t.album_id GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name

test_cases.question: List albums released after 2010
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT title, release_year FROM albums WHERE release_year > 2010

test_cases.question: Find the artist with the most tracks
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT a.name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count FROM artists a JOIN albums al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id JOIN tracks t ON al.album_id = t.album_id GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name ORDER BY track_count DESC LIMIT 1

test_cases.question: For each country show the number of artists and total number of albums
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT a.country, COUNT(DISTINCT a.artist_id) AS artist_count, COUNT(al.album_id) AS album_count FROM artists a LEFT JOIN albums al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id GROUP BY a.country

test_cases.question: Show all Rock albums with their artist names
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT al.title, a.name FROM albums al JOIN artists a ON al.artist_id = a.artist_id JOIN genres g ON al.genre_id = g.genre_id WHERE g.genre_name = 'Rock'

test_cases.question: How many albums are there?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM albums

test_cases.question: Find albums with only one track
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT al.title FROM albums al JOIN tracks t ON al.album_id = t.album_id GROUP BY al.album_id, al.title HAVING COUNT(t.track_id) = 1

test_cases.question: List the top 3 longest tracks
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT title, duration_seconds FROM tracks ORDER BY duration_seconds DESC LIMIT 3

test_cases.question: How many patients are there?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM patients

test_cases.question: List all doctors and their specialties
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT name, specialty FROM doctors

test_cases.question: Find all male patients
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT name, birth_date FROM patients WHERE gender = 'M'

test_cases.question: Show patients with blood type A
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT name, phone FROM patients WHERE blood_type = 'A'

test_cases.question: How many appointments did each doctor have?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT d.name, COUNT(*) AS appointment_count FROM doctors d JOIN appointments a ON d.doctor_id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.doctor_id, d.name

test_cases.question: What is the total treatment cost per doctor?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT d.name, SUM(a.treatment_cost) AS total_cost FROM doctors d JOIN appointments a ON d.doctor_id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.doctor_id, d.name

test_cases.question: Show all appointments in February 2024
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT p.name AS patient, d.name AS doctor, a.appointment_date, a.diagnosis FROM appointments a JOIN patients p ON a.patient_id = p.patient_id JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.doctor_id WHERE a.appointment_date BETWEEN '2024-02-01' AND '2024-02-29'

test_cases.question: Find the patient with the highest total treatment cost
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(a.treatment_cost) AS total_cost FROM patients p JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.name ORDER BY total_cost DESC LIMIT 1

test_cases.question: Show the most commonly prescribed medicine
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT medicine_name, COUNT(*) AS prescription_count FROM prescriptions GROUP BY medicine_name ORDER BY prescription_count DESC LIMIT 1

test_cases.question: Find patients who visited more than one doctor
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT p.name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.doctor_id) AS doctor_count FROM patients p JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.name HAVING doctor_count > 1

test_cases.question: Count patients by insurance type
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT insurance_type, COUNT(*) AS patient_count FROM patients GROUP BY insurance_type

test_cases.question: What is the average treatment cost?
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT AVG(treatment_cost) AS avg_cost FROM appointments

test_cases.question: Show total prescription cost per patient
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT p.name, SUM(pr.cost) AS total_rx_cost FROM patients p JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id JOIN prescriptions pr ON a.appointment_id = pr.appointment_id GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.name ORDER BY total_rx_cost DESC

test_cases.question: List doctors ordered by years of experience
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT name, specialty, years_experience FROM doctors ORDER BY years_experience DESC

test_cases.question: Find all prescriptions for patient Wang Lei
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT pr.medicine_name, pr.dosage, pr.duration_days FROM prescriptions pr JOIN appointments a ON pr.appointment_id = a.appointment_id JOIN patients p ON a.patient_id = p.patient_id WHERE p.name = 'Wang Lei'

test_cases.question: Find patients who had follow-up appointments
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.name FROM patients p JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id WHERE a.diagnosis LIKE '%Follow-up%'

test_cases.question: For each doctor show the number of unique patients, total appointments, and average cost
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT d.name, d.specialty, COUNT(DISTINCT a.patient_id) AS unique_patients, COUNT(*) AS total_appointments, AVG(a.treatment_cost) AS avg_cost FROM doctors d JOIN appointments a ON d.doctor_id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.doctor_id, d.name, d.specialty

test_cases.question: Count appointments by month in 2024
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT MONTH(appointment_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS count FROM appointments WHERE YEAR(appointment_date) = 2024 GROUP BY MONTH(appointment_date) ORDER BY month

test_cases.question: Find doctors who have prescribed the same medicine to multiple patients
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT d.name, pr.medicine_name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.patient_id) AS patient_count FROM doctors d JOIN appointments a ON d.doctor_id = a.doctor_id JOIN prescriptions pr ON a.appointment_id = pr.appointment_id GROUP BY d.doctor_id, d.name, pr.medicine_name HAVING patient_count > 1

test_cases.question: Show the average number of prescriptions per appointment
test_cases.gold_sql: SELECT AVG(rx_count) AS avg_prescriptions FROM (SELECT a.appointment_id, COUNT(pr.prescription_id) AS rx_count FROM appointments a LEFT JOIN prescriptions pr ON a.appointment_id = pr.appointment_id GROUP BY a.appointment_id) AS sub
